title: MEDIUM: No Content-Security-Policy header on HTML pages
description: The security_headers middleware in src/server.rs sets X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, and Referrer-Policy, but does not set a Content-Security-Policy header. HTML pages serve user-generated content (post bodies, display names, bios). Without CSP, if a sanitization bypass is ever found, there is no defense-in-depth against script injection. A restrictive CSP (e.g., default-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; script-src 'none') would mitigate this. File: /home/mark/PROJECT/smallhold/src/server.rs lines 38-81.
close_reason: Fixed: CSP header added for HTML pages (/@* and /oauth/authorize)
